_source: Ali, Dong & Lavely (2024) 'Irrigation scheduling optimization for corn yield' Ag Water Mgmt 306:109148; FAO-56 Ch 8+10; Stewart (1977)
_note: Benchmark for comparing irrigation scheduling strategies: rainfed, MAD-based, growth-stage, and ET-deficit. Uses corn (Ky=1.25) on silt loam with synthetic Michigan-like weather.

_provenance.method: Analytical: FAO-56 water balance + Stewart yield equation. Synthetic weather derived from Open-Meteo ERA5 climatology (East Lansing MI, 2023 growing season mean).
references: Ali, Dong & Lavely (2024) Ag Water Mgmt 306:109148
Allen et al. (1998) FAO-56 Chapter 8: Daily soil water balance
Allen et al. (1998) FAO-56 Chapter 10: Yield response
Stewart (1977) 'Optimizing crop production through control of water and salinity'
FAO-56 Table 24: Ky values by crop
_provenance._tolerance_justification: Mass balance: 0.01 mm (floating-point accumulation). Yield ratio: 0.001 (Stewart equation is exact for given inputs). WUE: 0.1 kg/ha/mm (sensitivity to strategy).

soil_parameters._source: USDA NRCS Soil Survey (silt loam, typical Michigan agricultural soil)

mad_50.description: Irrigate when depletion exceeds 50% of TAW (conservative)

mad_60.description: Irrigate when depletion exceeds 60% of TAW (moderate)

mad_70.description: Irrigate when depletion exceeds 70% of TAW (aggressive deficit)

growth_stage.description: Irrigate only during critical stages (tasseling + grain fill = mid-season)

mass_balance.description: Mass balance closure for each strategy (P + I = ET + DP + ΔS)

yield_ordering.description: More irrigation → less stress → higher yield (monotonic relationship)
yield_ordering.note: rainfed has most stress, mad_50 least stress. growth_stage targets critical period but allows stress outside it, so yield is between mad_70 and mad_60.

irrigation_totals.description: Total irrigation in realistic range for Michigan corn

stress_days.description: Rainfed has most stress days, fully irrigated has fewest

wue_optimization.description: WUE should peak at intermediate irrigation, not maximum irrigation
wue_optimization.note: Diminishing returns: over-irrigation wastes water without proportional yield gain

yield_physical_bounds.description: All yield ratios must be in [0, 1]

analytical_checks.description: Verify Stewart equation: Ya/Ym = 1 - Ky(1 - ETa/ETc)
